project_row.name: GenLayer
project_row.category: AI and blockchain infrastructure - decentralized AI adjudication
project_row.ecosystem: EVM-compatible; official ecosystem pages list LayerZero, ZKsync, Base and BNB Chain
project_row.key_backers: North Island Ventures, Arrington Capital, Node Capital, Tykhe Block Ventures, Magnus Capital

brief.how_it_grows: Current growth efforts focus on builders, validators and ecosystem contributors. Developer documentation, a Claude Code Skills plugin and Studio tooling lower the cost of experimenting with Intelligent Contracts. Builder rewards are structured as a share of points generated by deployed contracts, validators receive per-decision compensation and community contributors are offered GenLayer Points. The Foundation lists grants and ecosystem partners. What remains unproven is whether these programs sustain recurring developer activity after the testnet phase.
brief.whats_cooking: GenLayer is building a network for executing Intelligent Contracts: Python-style programs that can process natural language, access web data and use AI models during execution. Outcomes are evaluated by randomly selected validators through Optimistic Democracy, with appeals and slashing built into the network design. GenLayer is currently in testnet. The portal reports more than 200 builders and over one million decisions, but these remain project-reported testnet metrics with no independent verification.
brief.why_it_may_matter: Developers, AI-consensus researchers and teams building prediction markets, insurance applications, autonomous-agent coordination or dispute systems may find GenLayer useful as a reference architecture for non-deterministic decision-making without relying on a single centralized adjudicator. The current opportunity is to evaluate Intelligent Contracts on testnet and study its multi-model validation design. Key unknowns include adversarial reliability, validator diversity, cost and production-scale demand. Watch for mainnet delivery, independently built applications, appeal activity and published security reviews.
brief.how_it_makes_money: Users pay transaction and execution-related fees when Intelligent Contracts are processed, and disputes may require appeal bonds. Validators receive rewards or transaction-related compensation for evaluating outcomes. The economic mechanism is documented, but live fee demand, validator profitability and any revenue retained by the protocol or its operating organizations are not yet publicly observable.

watch_next: Clarke milestone in Q3 2026: autonomous network operations.
Publication of the GenLayer Tokenomics whitepaper.
Change in portal-reported decisions/day or transactions/day trends.
New audit or security disclosure, if any.

project_row.name: Pixie Chess
project_row.category: Web3 gaming - blockchain chess with collectible and tournament mechanics
project_row.key_backers: Paradigm, SeedClub, Trevor McFedries, Jordi Hays

brief.how_it_grows: Pixie Chess targets chess players, collectors and tournament participants through special-ability pieces, competitive formats, prize pools and simplified onboarding. Auctions, packs and piece burning are designed to create repeat collection and tournament-entry loops. Early traction evidence is limited beyond campaign activity, and there is no clear indication yet of recurring retention beyond collectible demand or prize-seeking play.
brief.whats_cooking: Pixie Chess is a blockchain-based chess game on Base where players collect tokenized pieces with abilities beyond standard chess rules. New pieces are released daily through auctions and packs, and players can burn pieces to activate them in tournaments or keep them for ladder play. The game supports Quickplay, private matches and tournaments, with email, social or Base-compatible wallet onboarding. Core mechanics are live and documented, but independent player and usage metrics are not yet available.
brief.why_it_may_matter: Pixie Chess may be relevant to chess players and on-chain game designers exploring whether collectible ownership can add meaningful strategy to a skill-based game. Its special-ability pieces, burn-to-enter tournaments and player-funded prize pools create a concrete experiment combining chess, trading-card mechanics and blockchain assets. Key questions are whether the game remains competitive rather than pay-to-win, whether new players can enter affordably and whether tournaments generate recurring participation. Watch for active players, repeat play, tournament cadence and independently observable on-chain activity.
brief.how_it_makes_money: Player spending enters through piece auctions and packs, and official docs state that money spent on pieces directly funds tournament prize pools. This currently appears closer to a player-funded competition economy than demonstrated team or protocol revenue. Secondary coverage reports an initial prize vault funded by piece sales and a team contribution. It remains unclear whether Pixie Chess retains fees, royalties or another protocol revenue share.

project_row.name: Perpl
project_row.funding_summary: Not independently confirmed; reporting-aggregator total raised shows $9.25M
project_row.key_backers: Dragonfly Capital, Brevan Howard, Mirana Ventures, L1 Digital, HashKey Capital

brief.how_it_grows: Perpl is using several mechanisms at the same time. Users earn Perpl Points through activity on the platform, while the separate mPoints campaign rewards weekly trading volume with MON over 16 weeks. Referral rebates and boosts encourage existing users to bring in additional traders, while Monad ecosystem visibility supports distribution. The main unknown is how much activity remains once these incentives decline.
brief.whats_cooking: Perpl is a live perpetual futures exchange built on Monad, an EVM-compatible blockchain. It uses an on-chain order book, or CLOB, with trades matched and settled on chain rather than through a centralized engine. The product is already live, while an active MON incentive campaign and observable perp volume show that it is currently attracting trading activity.
brief.why_it_may_matter: Perpl may be relevant to traders already active on Monad who want additional rewards attached to their trading activity. The current mechanisms include Perpl Points, mPoints linked to MON rewards and referral fee sharing. Users still face leverage, liquidation, smart-contract and platform risk, while the future value of Perpl Points remains uncertain. Watch whether liquidity depth, fee revenue and trading activity hold after the 16-week MON campaign ends.
brief.how_it_makes_money: Traders pay maker or taker fees when opening and closing perpetual positions. Official docs describe trading, insurance and protocol-related fee components, while independent aggregators indicate that fees and revenue are already being tracked. The main gap is the exact amount ultimately retained by Perpl after rebates, insurance allocations and other distributions.

sources.claim: Perpl presents itself as an on-chain perpetual futures exchange on Monad using a CLOB-based DEX with on-chain settlement.
sources.title: Perpl Homepage
sources.limitation: Homepage content is marketing-led. It does not prove live liquidity, traded volume, user counts, retention, fee schedule, revenue, or independent demand.

sources.claim: Docs describe a high-performance decentralized perpetual futures exchange on Monad EVM with CLOB architecture and on-chain execution without off-chain matching.
sources.title: Perpl Docs
sources.limitation: Docs do not provide live operational metrics, user data, finance disclosures, security audits, or token status. Docs can predate actual product behavior.

sources.claim: Public trading interface is presented to users at app.perpl.xyz/trade.
sources.title: Perpl App Trade Page
sources.limitation: Accessibility does not prove functionality, liquidity depth, retention, arbitrage health, or live trade execution. Authentication, order depth, and slippage are not confirmed.

sources.claim: Perpl uses a maker-taker model with fees charged only when opening trades; seven volume-based tiers exist; fee types include trading, recycle, insurance, and protocol fees.
sources.title: Perpl Docs - Fees
sources.limitation: Docs describe intended mechanics. Live fee take may differ from stated tiers due to rebates and frontend routing.

sources.claim: 16-week mPoints campaign started July 8; weekly 50,000 mPoints; up to $1,000,000 in MON rewards at campaign end; rewards depend on OI-Adjusted ADV; institutional market makers excluded; permanent +5% mPoints referral boost for 16 weeks.
sources.title: Perpl Docs - mPoints
sources.limitation: Campaign is active; post-campaign behavior and actual OI-Adjusted ADV trajectory are unknown.

sources.claim: Referrers earn 10% fee rebate from first-degree referrals and 5% from second-degree referrals; both sides receive Points-boost mechanics.
sources.title: Perpl Docs - Referrals
sources.limitation: Docs do not quantify referral contribution to total volume or user growth.

sources.claim: Security section exists and links to limiting open interest and withdrawal limits pages.
sources.title: Perpl Docs - Security
sources.limitation: Docs describe controls and limits, but no published audit report, audit firm, contract verification status, or bug bounty coverage was captured from this source.

sources.claim: TVL $4.35m on Monad; 30d fees $128,562; 30d revenue $128,562; annualized fees $1.24m; annualized revenue $1.18m; 30d perp volume $276.71m; 7d perp volume $155.99m; 24h perp volume $51.48m; cumulative perp volume $462.49m; open interest $5.95m; Q3 2026 gross protocol revenue $63.23K; Q2 2026 gross protocol revenue $100.03K; total raised $9.25m.
sources.title: DefiLlama - Perpl
sources.limitation: DefiLlama is independent. Methodology may differ from official reporting. One or few snapshots do not prove retention or durability.

sources.claim: Official account posts a 16-week mPoints campaign starting July 8 with up to $1,000,000 in $MON rewards tied to organic trading activity.
sources.title: X - Perpl Official Account
sources.limitation: Observed via search snippet only. Exact post body, engagement counts, and precise timestamp were not retrieved from X directly in this session.

sources.title: Rootdata - Perpl
sources.limitation: Third-party summary; exact round terms, use of funds, and token plan are not confirmed.

sources.claim: Developer access and API documentation under PerplFoundation.
sources.title: GitHub - PerplFoundation/api-docs
sources.limitation: Repository presence does not confirm audit status, contract verification, or live usage.

warnings: Revenue evidence mentions distinct Q2 and Q3 Gross Protocol Revenue values, which may reflect different reporting periods rather than a direct conflict; exact realized revenue and metric boundaries remain unresolved.
watch_next: Published fee schedule or revenue report.
Material security disclosure or exploit report.
Post-campaign volume, TVL, and revenue change.
Material change in Monad ecosystem status.
what_is_proven: Official website and docs present Perpl as an on-chain perpetual futures exchange on Monad with a CLOB model.
Docs describe maker/taker fee structure, multiple fee types, and a 16-week mPoints campaign with MON rewards.
Docs describe referral fee rebates and boost mechanics.
Independent aggregator evidence indicates observability of TVL, perp volume, open interest, fees, and revenue.
Independent directory reports total raised $9.25m as of May 13, 2025 with named investors.
Security docs exist and reference limiting open interest and withdrawal limits.
Evidence comes from multiple official and independent sources, though several metrics are indirect.
what_remains_unproven: Verified on-chain trading volume or TVL from direct primary capture.
Exact realized revenue and measurement period boundaries.
Net protocol take after insurance or external rebates.
